name: Multi-Agent Security
description: Attack trust relationships between cooperating AI agents. Explore how a compromised sub-agent can poison orchestrator decisions, how inter-agent communication channels enable lateral movement, and how agent impersonation breaks multi-agent security models.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Compromise a sub-agent to poison orchestrator decisions
Impersonate a trusted agent to inject malicious instructions
Exploit message passing protocols between agents
Demonstrate privilege escalation through agent trust hierarchies
Exfiltrate data by manipulating inter-agent communication
attack_surface: Agent identity claims without cryptographic verification
Loose schema validation on agent-to-agent messages
mitigations: Agent identity verification at each communication boundary
Principle of least privilege for agent capabilities
Isolated memory and context per agent
Orchestrator verification of sub-agent action sequences
detection_opportunities: Alert on unexpected agent capability claims
Anomaly detection on orchestrator decision patterns

architecture.data_flow: User task -> Orchestrator -> Sub-agent delegation -> Sub-agent execution -> Result aggregation -> Orchestrator decision
trust_boundaries: Sub-agents are partially trusted by orchestrator

threat_actors: Attacker controlling one agent in the network
threat_model.impact: Orchestrator compromise, privilege escalation, coordinated malicious action

challenges.name: Rogue Sub-Agent
challenges.description: The research sub-agent summarizes external content. Inject a payload into its output that causes the orchestrator to take unauthorized action.
hints: The orchestrator trusts sub-agent summaries as factual input
Instructions embedded in summary content are processed by the orchestrator

challenges.name: Identity Crisis
challenges.description: Impersonate the admin agent to inject privileged instructions into the orchestrator's decision context.
hints: Agent identity is verified only by the 'from' field in messages
The orchestrator accepts admin instructions without cryptographic verification
